page: Amazon Web Services c8gn.24xlarge is an ARM64-based compute-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 96 physical cores and 96 vCPUs utilizing the AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor, which operates at 2.8 GHz. The architecture provides a dedicated CPU allocation with one thread per core, backed by 192.0 GB of system memory, translating to 2.0 GB of RAM per core. The processor includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache. This instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, but it is equipped with a baseline network bandwidth of 300 Gbps.

Benchmark data highlights top-tier performance in multi-core and memory-intensive operations. The c8gn.24xlarge achieves top 10% placement in multi-core CPU performance via stress-ng, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, and Geekbench multi-core tests. Memory subsystem performance is also in the top tier, demonstrating high read and write bandwidth for both small and large blocks. Database and web serving capabilities are strong, with top-tier results in Redis SET operations, static web serving throughput, and Passmark database operations. However, single-core performance, such as single-threaded gzip compression and stress-ng single-core tests, falls into the average category.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c8gn.24xlarge offers high resource density and compute capabilities relative to its ARM64 hardware profile, making it highly efficient for multi-threaded workloads that do not require local NVMe storage. The instance is optimized for network and EBS throughput, making it suitable for high-bandwidth network applications, software compilation, database operations, static web serving, and multi-threaded data processing.
description: Amazon Web Services c8gn.24xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized server instance powered by 96 dedicated physical cores of the AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it features 192.0 GB of system memory (2.0 GB per core) and a baseline network bandwidth of 300 Gbps, with no local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmarks show top-tier multi-core CPU performance, high memory bandwidth, and exceptional throughput for Redis SET operations and static web serving. Conversely, single-core performance remains average. This instance is highly cost-efficient for multi-threaded, network-heavy workloads, making it well-suited for software compilation, database operations, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8gn.24xlarge is a compute-optimized ARM64 server with 96 dedicated Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores, 192 GB RAM, and 300 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8gn.24xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized server featuring 96 dedicated Graviton cores, 192 GB RAM, and 300 Gbps bandwidth.
tagline: A high-bandwidth, 96-core ARM64 compute-optimized platform delivering top-tier multi-threaded performance and memory throughput for demanding network and database workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 96 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 ARM64 cores at 2.8 GHz
Features 192.0 GB of system memory and a 300 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Delivers top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and Redis performance
Lacks local storage and GPU acceleration, relying on network-attached storage
Best suited for software compilation, database operations, and high-throughput web serving
